content.problemStatement:
Kim has successfully broken into the vault. He finds the main computer, a hideous creation with a wormhole to some extradimensional space.

As we all know, Kim is a very powerful hacker. However, it seems that he has finally met his match: Jishnu's computer network is too difficult for him to hack into, even though it is right in front of him! He needs your help to recover the files related to the JSA's world domination plans so he can expose them to the world and win the Pulitzer Prize.

Using some tricks, Jishnu has created a computer system with infinite memory! Each memory address is numbered from 111 to ∞\infty∞.

Kim knows that all of the data related to Jishnu's world domination plans is stored in NNN memory addresses. The iiith memory address is A[i]A[i]A[i]. These memory addresses may be very large, so we can express an integer XXX as the product of K(X)K(X)K(X) prime factors raised to some power. The prime factors are given in increasing order. Formally, for an integer XXX, there are two K(X)K(X)K(X)-length arrays P(X)P(X)P(X) and Q(X)Q(X)Q(X) where P(X)[i]<P(X)[i+1]P(X)[i] < P(X)[i+1]P(X)[i]<P(X)[i+1] for all iii, and X=∏i=1K(X)P(X)[i]Q(X)[i]X = \prod_{i=1}^{K(X)} {P(X)[i]}^{Q(X)[i]}X=∏i=1K(X)​P(X)[i]Q(X)[i]. This is the format that the input will be given in. Note that they may not be distinct.

Of course, it takes time to move between memory addresses. Kim has found an odd rule for finding the time needed to move between two memory addressees numbered XXX and YYY, dist(X,Y)dist(X,Y)dist(X,Y). Let each integer XXX be associated with an infinite-length sequence S(X)S(X)S(X). Below is the pseudocode for creating the sequence given XXX:

`S(X) = []
for i=1 to K(X) do:
    for j=1 to Q(X)[i] do:
        append P(X)[i] to S(X)
while (true):
    append -1 to S(X)

`Let VVV be the first index (0-indexed) where S(X)[V]≠S(Y)[V]S(X)[V] \neq S(Y)[V]S(X)[V]=S(Y)[V]. Furthermore, let size(X)size(X)size(X) be ∑i=1K(X)Q(X)[i]\sum_{i=1}^{K(X)} Q(X)[i]∑i=1K(X)​Q(X)[i]. Then, dist(X,Y)=size(X)+size(Y)−2∗Vdist(X,Y) = size(X)+size(Y)-2*Vdist(X,Y)=size(X)+size(Y)−2∗V. Please note, dist(X,X)=0dist(X,X) = 0dist(X,X)=0 because Kim is not moving memory address.

Kim's hacking works as follows: he chooses a starting memory address as his incision point, and then he goes directly to each  of the memory addresses which contains the world domination plans separately, one after another. So, the total amount of time it takes for Kim to get all the information is ∑i=1Ndist(C,A[i])\sum_{i=1}^{N} dist(C,A[i])∑i=1N​dist(C,A[i]), where CCC was his starting memory address. Kim is interested in what the minimum possible value of this could be if he chooses an optimal value of CCC. Since CCC must be a valid memory address, CCC must be an integer greater than or equal to 111.

###Input:


The first will contain one integer, NNN.
For the next NNN lines, the (i+1)(i+1)(i+1)th line will have their first integer as K(A[i])K(A[i])K(A[i]). Following that is 2∗K(A[i])2*K(A[i])2∗K(A[i]) integers, P(A[i])[1]Q(A[i])[1]...P(A[i])[K(A[i])]Q(A[i])[K(A[i])]P(A[i])[1] \quad Q(A[i])[1] \quad ... \quad P(A[i])[K(A[i])] \quad Q(A[i])[K(A[i])]P(A[i])[1]Q(A[i])[1]...P(A[i])[K(A[i])]Q(A[i])[K(A[i])].
###Output:
For each testcase, output one integer: the minimum possible value of ∑i=1Ndist(C,A[i])\sum_{i=1}^{N} dist(C,A[i])∑i=1N​dist(C,A[i]).

###Constraints


∑i=1NK(A[i])≤106\sum_{i=1}^{N} K(A[i]) \leq 10^6∑i=1N​K(A[i])≤106
1≤N≤1061 \leq N \leq 10^61≤N≤106
A[i]>1A[i] > 1A[i]>1 for all iii
P[i],Q[i]≤109P[i], Q[i] \leq 10^9P[i],Q[i]≤109 for all iii
P(X)[i]P(X)[i]P(X)[i] is always prime
P(X)[i]<P(X)[i+1]P(X)[i] < P(X)[i+1]P(X)[i]<P(X)[i+1] for all iii
All P(X)[i]P(X)[i]P(X)[i] are distinct for a given XXX.
###Subtasks


50 points : Q(A[i])[j]=1Q(A[i])[j] = 1Q(A[i])[j]=1 for all (i,j)(i,j)(i,j).
50 points : No additional constraints.
###Sample Input:
5
1 3 1
2 2 1 3 1
1 3 2
2 3 1 5 1
1 2 1

###Sample Output:
7

###EXPLANATION:
The array AAA is [3,6,9,15,2][3, 6, 9, 15, 2][3,6,9,15,2]. It can be verified that the minimum possible time is 777, which is achieved when C=3C=3C=3.
